steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to compare Fodor's hypothesis with Carruthers's. Key difference from the notes: Fodor says only low-level systems are modular; Carruthers (MMH) says all cognitive systems are modular.

steps.title: Match the goal to a choice
steps.content:
Choice A directly compares the scope: Fodorian modularity covers "some but not all" cognitive systems and is therefore "not as expansive" as MMH. B and D are sequential descriptions, not comparisons; C says they differ on low-level systems — but both consider low-level systems modular, so that's wrong.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
